title: Dense Smoky Algerian Sloe Berry Tagine
description: A rich and comforting Algerian dish featuring sloe berries and a smoky flavor, perfect for a sick day. This ayurvedic rajasic friendly recipe is based on a mother sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and cumin, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sloe berries, tomato paste, and vegetable broth, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Uncover the tagine and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs or a dollop of yogurt if desired.
